artPrompt: A widescreen film-still of a sly goblin rogue snatching a bowl of chips away from an outraged onlooker in a chaotic night-time town square, his triumphant smirk lit by leaping bonfire flame, the victim's hand grasping at empty air; embers, spilled snacks, and confetti scatter through the smoke, overturned boxes behind in flame orange against night-black haze, gleeful petty sabotage, rendered as gritty cinematic digital concept art — highly detailed, dramatic photoreal lighting, atmospheric haze, realistic textures.
name: Snack Break Saboteur
text:
When Snack Break Saboteur enters the battlefield, the most recently snacking player must put the snack down until your next turn. They may protest. It changes nothing.
Whenever a player reaches for a snack, you may roll a six-sided die. On a 5 or 6, that snack is now your snack. Take it. (You may not actually eat it without their consent. We are not monsters.)
flavorText: The chips were a casualty of governance.
